channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 100 replies — 397.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 100 • Updated: 2026-06-09 16:31 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62326539
>>62309503
Heavy bag huh?

62326550
>>62311487
ladies and gentlemen

the future of currency

62327899
>>62326531
this guy fucks

62328894
You are missing the most obvious bottom in history. And you are proud of it.

Grim.

62328962
>>62309503
are you dumb or something mate what the fuck the thing went to 58k already

62329036
>>62328962

THIS

op confirmed tard

62329055
>>62309503
intrinsic value doesn't exist
bitcoin is going to dozens of millions but for now the end of the bear cycle is not over and the bottom will likely be end of year

62329158
>>62309515
Buy the fucking dip and stake the little you can, you can never catch the bottom. If you panic sell, it will start pumping.

62329178
I hope this thread can survive another week or two, so we can laugh at OP when Bitcoin dumps to $50k

62329324
>>62329055
There is no bear cycle, it is just retards fire selling so they have free cash to buy into SpaceX IPO and end up inevitably rugged.

The BUYROG will win in the end. I hope this thread stays up until the end od the month, when BTC is at 75k and trending upwards because S&P refused to steal people's retirement to buy the latest AI scam.

62329342
>>62329324
Man, I hope you're right
It's been 8 straight months of dumping, need to see some green for a change

62329345
>>62329324
>52% drop over the last 8 months, with lower and lower bottoms over time
>"There is no bear cycle"

62329476
>>62329342
Serious, dude, just ignore these people (bear posters are just /smg/ bots and /pmg/ twofaced assholes, go tend to your garden, and come back in a few weeks.

The last time it was the same shit. The BUYROG called the bottom, they all laughed and posted bears, then the bottom was kept, and BTC rallied to near 80k before the SpaceX IPO prepping started.

We are frankly better off without these people. BTC is literally outperforming silver and 90% of crypto right now, yet they act like this is the end of crypto (it is not).

The idea of crypto is what matters, and they hate and fear this idea because they will lose, as they have lost many times before. Just zoom out the price charts.

This is not buy x sell, it is good x evil, and we are better off without retards buying into BTC so they can pump and dump.

62329478
>>62309503
/biz/ tricked me into waiting for a lower dip during the last bottom. I'm going to DCA now

62329970
>>62309503
Dunno m8 at my local groxery store they dib't accept bitcoin. Where xan you buy groceries, other than your cherrypicked example?

62331787
>>62329478
/biz/ always tells you to buy the dip. Then the dip comes, and they tell you not to buy it.

62331939
Brought to you by Epstein and Co.: Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value!

62333686
>>62309503
I bought the rally (my first cycle) and the dips/bottom. waiting on the good days now

62333871
>>62309503
30k

62334072
63K REJECTED

62334365
>>62309503
>65K is the bottom
But it went lower anon?

62334381
>>62329970
you can buy anything you want with a coinbase card, what're you on about geez?

62336135
THE BUYROG IS LAUGHING AT ALL OF THE BEAR SHILLS

YOU LOST. YOU SOLD THE BOTTOM

THE BUYROG WINS AGAIN

BEARSBANE

62336205
>>62309503
At least until it dips again. 26K is the real bottom, screen cap this.

62336221
>>62336205
It is never going 55k. I have no idea why PM metal shills (most of the bear is PM fags, we tested it) watch silver get ended by the Fed, and decide to lash out trolling people's financial lives.

I am glad the BUYROG convinced some people to not miss yet another dip.

62336238
>>62336135
>THE BUYROG IS LAUGHING
>still below the 65k "bottom" of OP

62337880
>>62336238
YOU LOST

62340948
Bear bros, our response? The buyrog keeps calling it right.

62341211
>>62340948
Sorry, I can’t hear anything with the noise of all these coins crashing at the same time.

62341507
>>62341211
just bought $20k MSTY

item.title: How do you actually cash out? (a large amount) — 44 replies — 176.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do you actually cash out? (a large amount)

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-06-09 16:33 UTC

OP

I'm planning on selling a decent chunk of my stack next year in order to pay for house construction.

Has anyone sold a large amount OTC and had the funds deposited in a US or European bank account? Any tips?

I'm talking 6 to 7 figures

Early replies (first 3)

62336656
>>62336622
cash out?

62336664
>>62336622
You talk to a tax consultant and let your bank know in advance that you have big crypto deposits coming in and to not flag your account

62336685
>>62336664
thanks, but how do you convert the crypto in the first place? OTC company, crypto exchange, something else?

do you know if some banks are more tolerant than others? i have accounts in the US and Europe

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62336892
>>62336664
Basically this
Unless you're explicitly trying to dodge taxes, which you can't do past cashing out above a certain amount, there's no issues in selling crypto
All the horror stories around here are from dishonest scammers that aren't telling they got their crypto from very poor opsec crime

62336920
Oh yes, absolutely. Because nothing says "carefully planned home construction" quite like tying your entire building budget to one of the most volatile asset classes on Earth and then assuming every intermediary involved will roll out a red carpet while a mountain of money teleports into your bank account.
In this magnificent circus act, the market will obviously cooperate, your chosen exit date will coincide with peak prices, the OTC desk will operate with the speed of a Formula 1 pit crew, your bank's compliance department will greet the incoming wire with confetti cannons, and tax authorities will simply nod approvingly from a distance. What could possibly go wrong?
The fantasy gets even better. You're building a house, a project famous for never experiencing delays, cost overruns, contractor disputes, permit issues, material shortages, or surprise expenses. Pair that with an asset that can decide to impersonate a falling piano at any moment, and you've created the financial equivalent of juggling chainsaws while riding a unicycle across a tightrope made of spaghetti.
Then comes the banking adventure. People talk about large transfers as if they're ordering a pizza. In reality, moving significant sums often involves documentation, source-of-funds checks, compliance reviews, questions, more questions, and occasionally questions about the questions. Yet somehow the expectation is that a giant OTC sale will produce a magical wire transfer that glides through the global financial system like a bald eagle soaring through a sunset.
The best part is the assumption that next year will conveniently be the perfect time to sell. Not six months earlier. Not six months later. Exactly when the concrete needs pouring and the invoices arrive. That's not financial planning; that's consulting a crystal ball while wearing clown shoes.
By all means, have an exit strategy.

62337140
>>62336656
this reminded me of 2017 when crypto was being talked about in the mainstream now and people started asking about whether or not you could actually cash out of crypto lol

62337148
>>62336873
God bless your soul

62337230
>>62336892
the issue will be with the bank that will freeze the account and question you like a criminal. should be resolvable but still a pain in the ass and lets you know who really is the one in control

62337875
>>62336622
the Jews will never allow it

62337902
>>62336920
>an asset that can decide to impersonate a falling piano at any moment
made me chuckle

62337909
>>62336920
funny clanker

62337949
>>62336622
I had huge issues cashing out. Tried to sell on the same exchange where I bought years ago and they wouldn't let me. Beside all the kyc bullshit, had to prove the source of the initial funds, even though they let me buy with no questions asked back then. A nightmare.

62338205
>>62336763
Took out 100k in the summer via Kraken to Revolut. No questions asked at all

62338346
>>62338205
part of making it is triggering the automated unusual activity detections that lead to interrogations or account suspensions on those less caring about individual customers

62338356
>>62336664
I didn't even tell the bank. Just transferred $300K straight from coinbase. They didn't even call like they have for some other large deposits. I did let the accountant know upfront but only because I had to talk with him about some other stuff. If your bank gives you shit about having money then you should get another bank.

62338965
>>62336622
I cashed out mid 6 figure via coinbase, transfer to my regular local bank. It was literally nothing, I did go see bank branch manager and told the the deposit was Coming and wanted to no fuckery, he understood but also said it was literally no issue.
After I did this, they started and have continued to give me all my deposits from job immediately, they don't sit on them for days like they do to all the plebs.
I hate banks so much.

62338979
I cashed about 80k from coinbase, not the same as your numbers but zero issues here. Didn't tell anyone I was doing it, no one cared, I figured I am a small fry.

62338985
>>62336622
You can’t cash out. Every major CEX will freeze your account immediately when you try and you’ll lose your money. They’ve done it for less. The only way is use multiple Asian banks at 20k a piece. Don’t even risk sending more than 10k to goybase a year

62338990
>>62338985
Why do I always see these posts? Who makes them? Simply not true and I don't believe you've experienced this.

62339110
>>62338990
It's true tho. The bank just denies the transaction and it's unable to be recovered.

62339183
>>62336622
depends on the liquidity. if it's some shitcoin with crappy liquidity you either want to slowly swap or otc. if it's eth or something you should not even be worried about cashing out 6 to 7 figures. just find the exchange with the lowest fees, dex or cex. if you can swap it to usdc on a dex for less fees than selling on a cex you can then send it over to coinbase to cash out with no fee.

62339195
>>62339110
This. Do it piece by piece.

The banks know crypto will kill them, and they've paid regulators to go along with it.

They can just block the transaction, and the exchange won't give a fuck and say it's gone.

62339234
>>62336622
when you want to cash out, you switch to DAI or USDT on TRX. then you find someone you trust locally that wants to give you $ in person. that's it.

62339239
>>62336920
>that's not x, it's x
ai posters should be maimed and tortured

62339497
UHH GUYS I CAN'T CASH OUT

62339725
>>62339497
Don't worry. You are the kind of trader who doesnt need to cash out

62339749
>>62339110
>>62339195
>your money will just like dissapear
Again, 1pbtid making up shit..

62339794
>>62336622
>Any tips?
Trocador and Crypto ATMs.

62340092
>>62336622
Get a Uphold debit card and spend it through that. You get 2% back in crypto. If you pay $100 you will get 4% back in crypto instead which is worth it at that level of purchase. Or buy gold with it and collateralize the loan against it.

62340190
>>62336685
Obviously you need to sell for cash first. Don’t just place a $800k sell order unless it’s Bitcoin on Coinbase and even then I’d do 8 orders of $100k separately. Use the biggest exchange you can. You don’t need OTC for sub 7 figures.

62341119
>>62337230
Banks will question you like a criminal no matter what. They're not good faith actors in any arena
>t. Had to get interrogated over 4 figures of a xmas gift from my grandmother a few years ago

62341409
>>62338965
He didnt ask for you to prove where it came from?

62341527
>>62341119
Ever heard about AML/KYC compliance? It’s not like the banks don’t enjoy taking your money. Governments strong-armed banks into playing bad cop and snitch with their own customers.

This way, governments are effectively outsourcing law enforcement to banks, as well as lawyers and accountants, while *getting paid* from these professionals.

The problem is the hubris of big government, as always.

item.title: ICP — 36 replies — 144.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ICP

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-06-09 16:42 UTC

OP

>we have steve jobs at home

Early replies (first 3)

62336952
>>62336934
soon

62337106
>>62336934
ICP has the best tech

62337150
>>62337106
ICUP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62337283
>>62337255
It's funny you say cause Bill gates is just a parasite who sucks off value and gives people aids, yeah you may be right, he is the next rat gates

62337316
>>62337236
I remember in 2000s when Amazon was down 99% in stocks while they were fully operational and people just like you tried to demoralize investors.

62337352
>>62337255
Bill Gates fucked a Russian hooker. Honestly based

62337356
>>62337316
It's been 6 years, faggot, 6 years and nothing to show for it, amazon gave back some profits post crash after 3 years, nobody is profitable on this piece of shit

62337365
>>62337356
>nothing to show for it

1. ICP was launched 5 years ago not 5
2. Caffeine.AI subscriptions are up 30% this month.
3 Partnerships with the UN, Switzerland, Pakistan, HBAR, Maestro etc..
4. Chain fusion

62337372
>>62337356
UN

62337376
>>62337316
I am ICP Chad but let’s not lie. Amazon went down ~90% after Dotcom crash and only once. ICP has gone down ~90% many times over (scam pump followed by crash) and is now down ~99% from ath. ICP is being repressed 10x more than Amazon because it is 10x a bigger deal

62337385
>>62337376
That’s because you’re ill informed. $700 was never the ATH, it was more like $80.
Hunchbacks used futures to manipulate the price at launch. That $700 literally lasted for a few hours before they deliberately crashed it.

62337420
>>62337376
Forget back -90%, nobody profited from this since Moonman shilled this around $50, the last semi good pump was from $3 to $20, that was the last pump, the somehow tech couldn't keep any pump because the rat of switzerland just keeps extracting, OGs were raped, early adopters never got anything back

62337568
>>62337420
Your obsession with moonman is actually a mental illness.
ICP is now far more advanced and mature than it was in 2024 when we reached almost $20.
The next pump will be much more extreme because ICP has had many adoptions this year.
You need to stop fudding, it’s really not healthy for you.

62337596
>>62337420
Also, mission 70 already cut inflation by 40%. The tokenomics are much better now than when ICP was worth $50. We just need an alt season to get this party going.

62337713
>>62337372
>icy pee is partnered with the foremost advocate of white genocide
bearish

62337760
>>62336934
I am considering dumping 10k into this. It might fail spectacularly but it’s the lowest MC and highest potential coin I can think of. I am tired of working myself to death.

62337787
>>62336934
Dis-lui que je lui passe le bonjour

62337830
>>62337760
That’s a good mindset. Worst case scenario you lose a few thousand dollars or you make half a million when the AI alt season starts.

62337832
>>62337385
I understand the futures Sam Barkman scam. I was going off a $200 top. Even $80 top puts ICP down 97% however

62337836
>>62337760
10k is a good gamble for something that has potential to retire you, just don’t over extend because the risk is big. ICP is imo still the most exciting narrative besides chainlink in crypto right now

62337916
>>62337836
Chainlink is not that exciting lol

62337929
>>62336934
Lol the black turtleneck lives on

62337937
>>62337760
For your own good, don't, trust me, i've been suckered into this scam for 6 years, it has fundamental issues stemming from it's core team, they are ok with the price hovering low just surviving and extracting

62337958
>>62337787
are you talking about mm?

62338026
>>62337937
I’ve held on and off for years as well. But seems like things are starting to click now.

62338066
>>62338026
Don’t listen to him, he’s a known fudder on biz, he hated ICP for years, his obsession with moonman is a mental illness.

62338597
>>62337916
Chainlink is very exciting, they research so much

62338901
>>62336934

62339051
>>62336934
>his first name on his handle is in lower caps
Woah these zoomers are so young and hip. I bet he's like 21 or something.

62339665
please fuck my ass

62339717
>>62337372
Looks like the entire top 30 is in that same list. What the fuck are you doing.

62340134
>>62339717
Dfinity is the most important because they have a partnership with the government of Pakistan a country with 100 million people.

62341606
>>62336934
.

item.title: “The keys was stolen” it was not our fault…. — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
“The keys was stolen” it was not our fault….

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-09 16:38 UTC

OP

It is obvious this is just a lie for their insider rugpull. It’s crazy how in crypto companies can just lie and say some bullshit like they were hacked when it’s always obvious it was an insider job to extract cash out and dump on retail to absolve themselves from any criminality or lessen the fud of an actual company sell off.

Vesting, etc, nothing actually matters because of this ability for companies to be able to make up any bullshit they want for an exit scam. So when you buy something in crypto how do you actually know that you are safe from it?

EdgeX crashed 50% in a day and they simply blamed market manipulation
Today Shariah AI dumped over 50%
Humanity Protocol dumped like 80% in a day
ZEC an exploit was found casing a crash

These crazy crashes are really upping in frequency. Is this a sign of something???

Early replies (first 3)

62341038
plausible deniability
you always need to treat these projects the same as solana memes

62341048
>>62340963
Literally who? Nobody? Ok.

62341063
>>62340963
>These crazy crashes are really upping in frequency. Is this a sign of something???
These are all cz exit liq hunting, where were u in the past 9 months?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62341072
>>62341063
I literally told you 500 tines in the past two weeks, go buy spacex ipo! Donut buy eth and btc under 1500/55k!

62341265
>I want p2p libertarian internet money where everyone can just do whatever the fuck they want and no central authority can control it!
>w-w-wait no not like that!

62341289
>>62340963
If you invested in a fucking shitcoin named "Humanity" (literally a name that screams ABORT SCAM SCAM RED ALERT), you deserve everything that happened to you desu.

62341331
>>62341289
In the same vein, as soon as I saw Bankman-Fried talking about his "effective altruism" and how he was such a humble guy driving a shitty Toyota (in the Bahamas) after making his fortune in "arbitrage", I knew it was time to invest with Kraken instead. One month later FTX was bust.

62341442
>>62341331
I knew Sam Barkman was not to be trusted from the first time I heard about him because he is an ugly goblin kike. But yeah, virtue signalling is also a massive red flag.

62341569
>>62340963
>So when you buy something in crypto how do you actually know that you are safe from it
in the past people would verify the smart contract and would, at the very least, avoid buying things where the mint function was still accessible in any way. but nowadays crypto devs are retarded and lazy and put everything under at best a multisig, at worst a single key that then gets mysteriously """exploited""". It doesn't help that centralized bridges and centralized stablecoins are entrenched as the norm, which is probably why all these lazy ass former web2 devs don't give a fuck and run incredibly insecure setups, because why bother pretending it's all decentralized when 99% of liquidity depends on centralized entities claiming they have the funds backing their shit

62336790

item.title: Canadian job market — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Canadian job market

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-09 16:46 UTC

OP

it's so cooked holy shit. I've been applying to jobs for 2 months now and no one is returning. I've been spending hours making custom resumes and cover letters. I'm overqualified in most cases too. NO ONE HAS RETURNED.

Early replies (first 3)

62341141
>>62341035
>I've been applying to jobs for 2 months now and no one is returning
7 months here, 3 interviews, time to start goofmaxxing

62341445
>>62341035
Day of the rake has arrived

62341463
your domain?
job title you are aiming for?
years of experience?
salary expectation?
education?
certifications?

im hr in quebec

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62341598
>>62341035
All low level work prefers jeets because gov subsidizes their wage and they only have to pay them half, all medium to high end work requires nepotism, if no connections then they value DEI, only jobs available to white people are local smaller companies that don’t have to play by dei rules. Unfortunately these companies tend to be very toxic and upwards mobility is impossible unless you can make friends with the manager or boss, all that matters is charisma. You work hard and do your job well? No promotions for you, they will keep you where you are because why move you from a position where you make the owners a lot of money

62341640
>>62341035
Took me 6 months straight out of uni with an engineering BSc degree in 2023. That was pre-AI in Europe. Good luck.

62341662
>>62341035
Canada hasn't had a real job market since 2008. The market for helping people find jobs is bigger than the actual job market. There was a small window of opportunity right after covid when a bunch of boomers retired and companies had to hire due to severe staffing shortages, but after that it's been nothing but only jeets getting jobs.

This entire economy is captured, no one here does anything of value or makes an attempt to improve anything. The last time anyone invested in technology at all was in the 1960-80s. I work for the government and we still buy newly manufactured goods from the 80s because some boomer business got into the approved goy list back then and pulled the ladder up behind them.

If you are young and have the means, it is in your best interests to leave this god forsaken land as soon as possible or it will be the end of your bloodline.

item.title: QQQ vs. VOO / AKA Not Squandering a Bull Run While Young — 8 replies — 17.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
QQQ vs. VOO / AKA Not Squandering a Bull Run While Young

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-09 16:19 UTC

OP

>Been DCA into VOO for a fair bit now
>Realize I shouldn't squander this bull run and at least take an Index / ETF with more volatility if I don't wanna bet on individual stocks like Sandisk or Nvidia
>Look at QQQ vs VOO real returns over time
>QQQ and VOO just mirror each other, with some years performing the same, otherwise QQQ has almost exactly twice the gains and twice the loses (by % not year count) as VOO
As someone who is only 26, why would I not just go into the more volatile option here that still gives more returns
Is QQQ way too tech focused right now, not that VOO isn't tech focused, but I heard the Nasaq was bending the rules to allow these AI companies in

Please reply with actual advice, analysis, your own portfolios as examples, alternatives to these two without betting the farm, etc. I just wanna DCA my paychecks into shit without feeling like I missed the boat or FOMO (not that I have that much)

Much appreciated.

Early replies (first 3)

62338683
>>62338660
you already missed a lot of the difference. That said I'm still buying QQQ rather than other index funds bc everyone thinks we're already at the top of the tech bubble. Also don't make the noob mistake QQQM is technically better for longterm holding but it's marginal really

62338807
>>62338660
>only 26
You should be going deep on 0DTE 20% OTM calls on NVDA earnings

62338873
>>62338807
why chase premiums? legit, why
>>62338683
sadly just wasn't fully employed until a year and a half ago.....

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339090
Bump

62340367
QQQ is going to tilt you in the direction of the tech boom
VOO also includes you in this since they’re on the S&P anyway
It’s more growth but more volatility
QQQ is just a bias towards growth and VOO is a bias towards total market exposure
As long as those top companies remain heavily skewed in their profitability then go with QQQ
Or go with both
VOO and chill exists for a reason then it’s probably better like look into other ETFs that focus on a particular sector if you wanted more growth

62340967
>>62338660
If you want to see why, look back fsrther than 2011.

62340975
>>62340967
And i dont just mean 2008 either. Nasdaq got turbofucked in the ass with a sawzall in 2001 compared to the sp

62341400
>>62340967
>>62340975
I went back and compared CAGR and it just seems like a wash based on how much volatility you want

item.title: I bought a few thousand dollars worth of LINK immediately after seeing Sergey shaking hands with Trump. — 8 replies — 8.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
I bought a few thousand dollars worth of LINK immediately after seeing Sergey shaking hands with Trump.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-09 15:50 UTC

OP

I bought a few thousand dollars worth of LINK immediately after seeing Sergey shaking hands with Trump. I've lost so much money since then, I can't believe I actually fell for it. I'm still mostly in just BTC but I actually got scammed, feels horrible man.

Early replies (first 3)

62335000
>buying when it's on the news

62335010
>>62335000
trips of wisdom

62335027
>>62334973
First of all ure gambler so u deserve to lose everything. Second of all, you never buy on the news - you invest (youre gambling not investing so this does not concern you) before news happen by using public info and calculating how good or bad news are plausible.
Third of all, next time look THEY WAY THEY SHOOK HANDS retard. Did this sergey cuckoldery look to you like Trump gave a flying shit about him? This was a sell signal if anything

62335038
>>62334973

>so much money
Pick one

62336812
>>62334973

You should have bought Polkadot

62340148
>>62334973
Chin up lad

62340299
>>62334973
You are waiting on the banks releasing their products ahead of the Clarity Act. There were two laws that needed to be passed before the value of those laws can be demonstrated, Genius was the first. Clarity is next, maybe this month. After that, banks can finally start using smart contracts in production environments and also the Tokenized real world asset market will explode.

If these two markets are everything you hear about in the news and Link's token price is still in the dumps, then you can panic. Link's token price was always suppressed to keep it out of the top 10. They're not going to let it rip till they have to since they don't scrubs like us owning a share of their financial network.

62341169
Buy the rumor.
Sell the news.

item.title: I honestly don't get — 6 replies — 7.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I honestly don't get

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-09 16:01 UTC

OP

how anyone defends CLL at this point. I say this as someone holding since 2017 who has accumulated over 60k stinks and largely still believes in the long term viability of the investment.

But CLL seems to be completely tone deaf with what people actually want to be hearing about. I get there might be NDAs or whatever agreements that they can't disclose details about certain relationships, but completely neglecting to talk about things like staking, go into details about the fee switches, etc. -- all shit that they have full control and say over -- despite everyone asking about it nonstop for fucking years, is just absurd. Even if they were scared to talk about anything due to legal reasons they could literally just say "Hey we hear you but we're not commenting on X, Y, or Z due to legal reasons at this time." but they don't.

The guys who white knight nonstop for them are not helping anything. Personally I do not post on X and so I am not vocal in either way but I really legitimately just don't understand who is DEFENDING them at this point. Can anyone explain it?

Early replies (first 3)

62340620
tl;dr - not sellng

62340627
>>62340616
Oh also the sweeping under the rug of the catastrophe that is BUILD. Like, just fucking acknowledge it as a failure. It's mostly not even their fault. 95%+ of those projects died gruesome deaths and the tokens are worthless.

Are you guys aware CLL took custody of the tokens for most of them already (and likely sold though I have no proof of that) but just never distributed? And now there's no point in distributing because the projects are mostly legitimately dead and there's 0 liquidity for the tokens.

Just fucking say "hey, our intentions were good but things went sideways and here's why and what we plan on doing about it", but they literally do not even acknowledge what an absolute shit show BUILD became.

62341186
no honest linkies are left on this board huh, just the deranged fuddie holders and a spare few dr;ns guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62341201
>>62340616
Nice blogpost

62341204
>>62341186
nigga you bought a 2017 premined shitcoin, what did you really expect?

62341249
>>62340616
Hey, respect other people's fetishes.

There's nothing wrong with getting turned on by donating money to a fat russian scammer.

OP

As the worlds collide, you'll see the new era being born. The New New World you'll see will hit you like a dream, as cash becomes something you never thought it could ever be. BELIEVE IN XNO!

Now this is what it's like when worlds collide

Now this is what it's like when worlds collide
Now this is what it's like

What is it really that's goin' on here?
You've got the system for total control
Now is there anybody out there?
Now watch us suffer, yeah, 'cause we can't go
What is it really that is in your head?
What little life that you had just died
I'm gonna be the one that's takin' over
Now this is what it's like when worlds collide
[Chorus]
Are you ready to go?
Cause I'm ready to go
What you gonna do, baby, baby?
Are you goin' with me?
Cause I'm goin' with you
That's the end of all time

[Verse 2]
What is it really that motivates you?
The need to fly or this fear to stop?
I'll go along, when you realize
When we get there, I say nine of ten drop
Now who's the light and who is the Devil?
You can't decide, so I'll be your guide
And one by one, they will be hand-chosen
Now this is what it's like when worlds collide

[Chorus]
Are you ready to go?
Cause I'm ready to go
What you gonna do, baby, baby?
Are you goin' with me?
Cause I'm goin' with you
That's the end of all time

[Bridge]
(You are a robot)
Now this is what it's like when worlds collide
Now this is what it's like
Now this is what it's like when worlds collide
Now this is what it's like

[Verse 3]
What is it really, when they're fallin' over?
Everything you thought was denied
I'm gonna be the one that's takin' over
Now this is what it's like when worlds collide

[Chorus]
Are you ready to go?
Cause I'm ready to go
What you gonna do, baby, baby?
Are you goin' with me?
Cause I'm goin' with you
That's the end of all time
Are you ready to go?
Cause I'm ready to go
What you gonna do, baby, baby?
Are you goin' with me?
Cause I'm goin' with you
That's the end of all time

item.title: Germany Is Primed For Economic Boom — 8 replies — 6.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Germany Is Primed For Economic Boom

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-09 15:32 UTC

OP

Germany is in the middle of the biggest economic shift since the invention of the steam engine and it is over the halfway mark: Energiewende.

Now, with clean efficient renewable energy Germany has began explosive investment into AI and modern tech industries. The largest economy in Europe and the third largest in the world has entered the ring against the US tech giants. With modern energy and while the rest of the world struggles with oil shortages and the Iranian crisis (and the Trump crisis), the timing could not be better.

There is often negativity surrounding Germany, both here and broadly in the media. However, these negative sentiments are often sensationalist and poorly considered. This video explains well why Germany is still in a fantastic economic position. He does not mention it, but the increased energy independence with cheap clean renewable sources is even more beneficial for manufacturing. The growing pains are over and now the goliath can awaken.
https://youtu.be/cg43s4PkPX0

This new green economy also creates a host of growth opportunities in renewables. Both domestically but also abroad when other countries are forced to go through the process Germany has in order to modernize their energy. Fossil fuels must end we cannot continue to rely upon them or the consequences will be disaster. Hopefully, Americans can be convinced of this before they doom us all.

Which also brings us to the last issue: PPP. Based on OECD data, equivalent prices in Germany would be 81% of what they are in America. Even this data fails to capture the entire story in my opinion because it is all prices. The reality is necessary goods like healthcare, food, and education are far cheaper in Germany while consumer goods are more similar. The result not only are Germans with the same income relatively richer in all areas but the economy is more equal as only luxury goods are truly expensive because there are not allowed these corporate monopolies on the basic needs of life.

Early replies (first 3)

62338713
>>62338691
Kek nice fanfiction

62338716
>>62338691
Europeans are too lazy, overregulated, and overtaxed to grow

62338787
Germany will never be allowed to boom, as punishment for WWII. Their resources will be extracted, but they won't see a red cent.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339978
>>62338716
Americans are lazy and fat but they steal wealth with their army.

62339986
>>62338691
bait used to be believable

62340965
Late stage soviet propaganda be like >>62338691

62341014
Yeah na, the biggest boom for germany would be the rest of europe beginning to pay their own bills.

62341040
Isn't Merz one of the lowest rated european leaders right now and the AfD hovering around 30% in the polls?
If Germany is doing so well why aren't people voting for the status quo?

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 175 replies — 124.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 175 • Updated: 2026-06-09 15:22 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62331850
>>62312653
LINK holders are the liberals of crypto, you're completely delusional.
You're on your way out though.

62332143
>>62331087
if miners stop the difficulty plummets
if miners want to support a fork they can redirect all the hashrate
are you legitimately retarded?

62332425
>>62332143
>if miners stop the difficulty plummets
Yes.
And the lower the lower the mining difficulty, the more smaller mining rigs can mine.

>if miners want to support a fork they can redirect all the hashrate
Yes, they'll redirect all their hashrate to a fork nobody will use.

You changed IDs for this? You fucking goofball.

62332438
All of you need to KYS’sselves

62332450
>>62332425
>miners can't decide shit
>eerrr they can but they won't
I accept your concession, keep moving the goalposts you fucking imbecile

62332473
>>62332450
>>eerrr they can but they won't

kek what the fuck are you talking about?

62332491
glad the chainlink general thread format is finding acceptance

62332578
>>62332491
You keep saying this, but it's pretty much just fuddies creating about 20 fudthreads and then bumping one ad nauseum. The amount of link fud threads on the board doesn't decrease because of this "general" at all. Maybe mods should just ban all LINK fud threads and corral them into a single fuddie general, it's beyond ridiculous now that the board is so slow.

62333330
>>62325222
Chainlink confidential compute for private tokenization too

62335182
>>62332578
shut up cuckold

price + chart :)

62335458
>>62335182
Thanks for bumping /CFG/ (Chainlink Fuddie General)

62336610
>>62332425
>You changed IDs for this? You fucking goofball.
Still here. Feel free to also refute my point on your BTC comparison falling apart due to the flow of change of hands from new BTC to node operators to market versus new Link tokens going from CLL's uncirculated supply directly to Binance instead of node operators.

62336627
>>62335458
p+c bitch

62336936
>>62316605
>Chainlink pays the nodes from their own team wallets

>>62336610
>Link tokens don't go from CLL's uncirculated supply to node operators

You're a fucking retard you know that.

62337435
>>62333330
Epstein should have stored his blackmail material in here amirite

62337475
If chainlink is decentralized why do not even the biggest, stinkiest linkies here brag about running a node?

62338077
>>62337475
linkpool made us think we could back in the day, it was all a scam, we all had a running node setup and we were waiting to be whitelisted, what a fking joke

62340215
>>62336936
You're misrepresenting my point to argue something else. Why are you doing this? You get nothing out of it unless you're a paid shill or just bored.

62340223
>>62337475
We tried. It's a full time job requiring a bunch of resources on top of the profits being nebulous thus souring a lot of people out of running their node [on AWS]. This was back in 2019 so I don't know how robust and improved running nodes is nowadays.

62340231
>>62340215
I argued against your point that "Chainlink paying the nodes from their own team wallets" decouples the link price from the usage; and then you literally asked me to refute your point that Chainlink doesn't pay the nodes from the uncirculating supply, and is instead sending the Link directly to Binance.

What the fuck are you even doing here?

62340287
>>62340231
> BTC
> No new BTC enters the market except through miners' hands.
> This means they decide and influence the price of BTC.
> This couples the BTC price to the usage of the network because miners will not sell less than the costs of operations

> Link
> new Link enters the market through payments to node operators, payments to developers, and to deals with companies
> Link node operators are no longer the sole arbiters who decide when new Link enters the market
> To access services from the network, you don't need to pay in Link, and your payments made aren't immediately converted to Link, if at all. This decouples the link token from accurately reflecting network activity
> Furthermore CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements, Ex: China's response to the Trump tariffs

https://etherscan.io/tx/0x6e347aa07b74b2330b8f3f5a9da44305cba0bf7e40b416bcf00ba9922c365fdd
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x3355d51eafaead23e063aba8a099a712a11ecb26e937f53d0850cd4c5b33801e
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x2e3b0111c94d9d0102e5822ccd9dc5f395c5b207b39a466432c1d634c4354eb6

^Above transactions took place just an hour before the great crash of October


Your job is to refute my conclusion that the link token price is NOT tied to network usage. Refute these points. You are free to disregard the BTC comparison since those are points in my favor unless you can refute my points. Any thing other than directly addressing and showcasing flaws in my logic (and I better hear something better than the low hanging fruit of "Node operators have to sell Link for cost operations too!") and I will respond with "token not needed".

62340304
>>62340287
You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.
This is what I refuted.
I pointed this out to you many times but you keep pretending not to understand or something.

62340325
>>62340304
>You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.

token not sneeded

62340347
>>62340325
>>62340287
>CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements
This is horseshit by the way.
He has always sold on Fridays, pretty much around the same hour, and always within 3 to 4 month intervals.

Sometimes Link pumps right after (many times in fact), sometimes some shit happens right after. But his timing is fully transparent.

62340403
>>62340347
Anon, the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do in response to Trump's tariffs. For the uninitiated, Trump proposed a 100% tariff on China and China responded with crashing the market. You can dig into the archives to verify. Type in either "Trump", "China" "Binance", "Link" or whatever and date it to October.

62340410
Fuck you chainlinkies!

Sincerely,

The whole XRP community

62340414
>>62340403
>the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do
Those links are the transactions Sergey made on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.
Which has been his unlocking regimen since forever.

62340536
>>62340287
If (almost) no one uses the network, will the token price change or remain (almost) unchanged?

62340953
>>62340347
>But his timing is fully transparent.


classic boyscout bold faced lie

there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales other than them doing it 4 times per year. often it will be completely different months as years past.

didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol

62340971
>>62340953
>there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales
- always around 7% of total supply annually
- always on Fridays
- always within 3 to 4 month intervals

item.title: Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. — 17 replies — 21.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-09 16:00 UTC

OP

Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. Look at this poster who copies and pastes an entire thread just to shit it up and make fun of XRP holders: >>62338460

The funny thing? He forgot to remove the (You) when he copied and pasted the whole thing. But you can see his ID is different.

So my best guess is it's probably a single poster who constantly switches id to force this shitty meme. Probably a chainlink bagholder.

62339778

62339781

Lol of course I don't care so much but it goes to show how pathetic those link tards are trying to force that unfunny boom-xrp meme. The guy thinks if he switches id it looks like more people are in on it but that retard forgot to remove the (You) from his own post.

You are just butthurt you got called out hard and i'm gonna show this to everyone on /biz/ so they know who is really responsible for forcing that dumb meme.
